title: Vault & Login
description: Store credentials securely in the local vault, then use them to log in to a site.

steps.content: The vault stores credentials locally using OS-level encryption (macOS Keychain, Windows DPAPI, Linux secret-service). Nothing leaves your machine.

steps.annotation: Store credentials in the encrypted local vault

steps.content:
[page snapshot]
  Sign In — Demo App
  ──────────────────
  Email:     [@ref=1] <input name="email" type="email">
  Password:  [@ref=2] <input name="password" type="password">
  [@ref=3] <input type="checkbox"> Remember me
  [@ref=4] <button type="submit"> Sign in

steps.annotation: Auto-login using vault credentials — detects fields and fills them
Verify we are now on the authenticated dashboard

steps.content:
[page snapshot]
  Dashboard — Demo App
  ────────────────────
  Welcome back, Alice!

  [@ref=1] <a> "My Projects" → /projects
  [@ref=2] <a> "Settings" → /settings
  [@ref=3] <a> "Team" → /team
  [@ref=4] <button> Sign out
Logged in successfully. The vault auto-detected the email and password fields, filled them from stored credentials, and submitted the form — all in one command.
